summary: Loan Application Service — Get Application Details Endpoint
description:
Implement the GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} endpoint in loan-service. This covers all work needed to retrieve full application details for a specific loan by ID: fixing the existing DocumentSummary typo in loanflow-common, adding the getApplicationById method to the service interface and a stub implementation that fetches from the database and enforces ownership for APPLICANTs, wiring the controller endpoint with role-scoped access, extending GlobalExceptionHandler with a 404 handler, and adding Redis caching so repeated reads are served from cache rather than hitting the database.

ApplicationDetailResponse, ApplicantDetailsSummary, and DocumentSummary already exist in loanflow-common. LoanApplicationRepository already has findByApplicantUserId. SecurityConfig and GlobalExceptionHandler were scaffolded in the create-loan-application epic — this epic extends them.

Common exceptions are centralised in loanflow-common under com.loanflow.common.exception so any service can import and throw them without duplicating exception class definitions. ResourceNotFoundException is defined there and imported by loan-service.
userType: backend developer owning the Loan Application Service
functionality: implement the get application details endpoint with ownership enforcement, a 404 handler, and Redis caching so the documented GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} contract is fully exercisable
benefit: so authenticated users can retrieve a full loan application by ID with correct role-scoped access control and cached reads before business logic implementation begins

stories.summary: Fix DocumentSummary typo and verify ApplicationDetailResponse shape in loanflow-common
stories.description:
DocumentSummary in loanflow-common has a field named 'verfied' (missing 'i'). The API reference and ApplicationDetailResponse both expect 'verified'. Fix the typo so the serialised JSON key matches the documented response shape.

Also confirm that ApplicationDetailResponse contains every top-level field in the API reference 2.2 response: applicationId, status, loanType, amount, termMonths, annualIncome, submittedAt, applicantDetails, documentSummary. The class already exists — this story validates it and corrects the only known gap.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: DocumentSummary serialises with the correct 'verified' field name
acceptanceCriteria.given: a DocumentSummary is built with required=4, uploaded=4, verified=4
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON contains the key 'verified' not 'verfied'

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ApplicationDetailResponse serialises all API reference 2.2 fields
acceptanceCriteria.given: a fully populated ApplicationDetailResponse is built with all fields set
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON contains applicationId, status, loanType, amount, termMonths, annualIncome, submittedAt, applicantDetails, and documentSummary at the correct nesting levels

tasks.description:
Edit backend/loanflow-common/src/main/java/com/loanflow/common/dto/application/response/DocumentSummary.java.

Rename the field 'verfied' to 'verified'. The field type remains int. Update the @Builder and constructor accordingly — Lombok regenerates these so only the field declaration needs changing.

Run mvnw clean install -DskipTests from backend/loanflow-common to confirm the module compiles after the rename.

stories.summary: Add getApplicationById to LoanApplicationService with ownership enforcement and 404 handling
stories.description:
Add the getApplicationById method to the LoanApplicationService interface and implement it in LoanApplicationServiceImpl. The implementation must:

1. Look up the LoanApplication by applicationId using the existing JpaRepository findById — throw ResourceNotFoundException if not found.
2. For callers with role APPLICANT, verify that the application's applicantUserId matches the caller's userId — throw AccessDeniedException if not.
3. Build and return an ApplicationDetailResponse from the entity. The documentSummary is stubbed with zeros for now (document counts come from document-service in a later sprint).

ResourceNotFoundException is defined in loanflow-common under com.loanflow.common.exception — it is not created in loan-service. Import it from loanflow-common. Add a @ExceptionHandler for it in the existing GlobalExceptionHandler that returns 404.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById throws ResourceNotFoundException for unknown applicationId
acceptanceCriteria.given: no LoanApplication exists with the requested applicationId
acceptanceCriteria.then: ResourceNotFoundException is thrown with a message identifying the applicationId

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById throws AccessDeniedException when APPLICANT requests another user's application
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists with applicantUserId 10 and the caller's userId is 99 with role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.then: AccessDeniedException is thrown and the application data is not returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById returns ApplicationDetailResponse for the owning APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists with applicantUserId 10 and the caller's userId is 10 with role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.then: an ApplicationDetailResponse is returned with applicationId, status, loanType, amount, termMonths, annualIncome, submittedAt, applicantDetails, and a stubbed documentSummary with zeros

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: getApplicationById returns ApplicationDetailResponse for LOAN_OFFICER without ownership check
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists with applicantUserId 10 and the caller has role LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.then: an ApplicationDetailResponse is returned without an ownership check being applied

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GlobalExceptionHandler returns 404 with standard error payload for ResourceNotFoundException
acceptanceCriteria.given: the controller calls getApplicationById and ResourceNotFoundException is thrown
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 404 response is returned containing timestamp, status 404, error 'Not Found', message, and path fields

tasks.summary: Add 404 handler for ResourceNotFoundException to GlobalExceptionHandler

stories.description:
Add the getApplicationById method to LoanApplicationController. The endpoint must:

1. Accept the applicationId path variable.
2. Extract callerUserId and callerRole from the JWT claims.
3. Delegate to LoanApplicationService.getApplicationById.
4. Return 200 with the ApplicationDetailResponse body.

Security is already enforced by SecurityConfig from the create-loan-application epic — all authenticated requests reach the controller. Ownership and role checks are enforced in the service layer.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a request is sent to GET /api/v1/applications/50021 with no Authorization header
acceptanceCriteria.when: the request reaches the security filter chain

acceptanceCriteria.given: no application exists with id 99999 and a valid JWT is provided
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 404 response is returned with the standard ApiErrorResponse payload

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} returns 403 when APPLICANT requests another user's application
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists for userId 10 and the caller's JWT carries userId 99 with role APPLICANT

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} returns 200 with ApplicationDetailResponse for the owning APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists for userId 10 and the caller's JWT carries userId 10 with role APPLICANT
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 response is returned with a body containing applicationId, status, loanType, amount, termMonths, annualIncome, submittedAt, applicantDetails, and documentSummary

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: GET /api/v1/applications/{applicationId} returns 200 for LOAN_OFFICER regardless of ownership
acceptanceCriteria.given: a LoanApplication exists for userId 10 and the caller's JWT carries any userId with role LOAN_OFFICER
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 response is returned with the ApplicationDetailResponse body

tasks.summary: Smoke test the GET endpoint via dev profile
tasks.description:
Start loan-service with the dev profile and confirm:

1. GET /api/v1/applications/50021 with no JWT returns 401.
2. GET /api/v1/applications/99999 with a valid JWT returns 404 with ApiErrorResponse.
3. GET /api/v1/applications/{id} with an APPLICANT JWT for a different user returns 403.
4. GET /api/v1/applications/{id} with the owning APPLICANT JWT returns 200 with a body containing applicationId, status, loanType, amount, termMonths, annualIncome, submittedAt, applicantDetails, and documentSummary.

stories.description:
Add Redis caching to LoanApplicationServiceImpl.getApplicationById so that repeated reads for the same applicationId are served from cache rather than hitting the database. Cache entries must be evicted when the application is updated.

Add spring-boot-starter-data-redis to loan-service pom.xml. Configure a CacheManager bean with a dedicated TTL for the application-details cache. Annotate getApplicationById with @Cacheable and any status-update method with @CacheEvict. Configure the Redis connection in application-dev.yaml.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Second call to getApplicationById for the same applicationId does not hit the database
acceptanceCriteria.given: getApplicationById has been called once for applicationId 50021 and the result is in cache
acceptanceCriteria.when: getApplicationById is called again for applicationId 50021
acceptanceCriteria.then: the repository findById is not invoked and the cached ApplicationDetailResponse is returned

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Cache entry is evicted when the application status is updated
acceptanceCriteria.given: a cached entry exists for applicationId 50021
acceptanceCriteria.when: the application status is updated via the service layer
acceptanceCriteria.then: the cache entry for applicationId 50021 is removed so the next read fetches from the database

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Cache miss for unknown applicationId still throws ResourceNotFoundException
acceptanceCriteria.given: no cache entry and no database row exist for applicationId 99999
acceptanceCriteria.then: ResourceNotFoundException is thrown and nothing is written to cache

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: loan-service starts with Redis unavailable and falls back gracefully
acceptanceCriteria.given: Redis is not running and spring.cache.type is set to 'none' in application-dev.yaml
acceptanceCriteria.then: the context loads without error and getApplicationById serves responses directly from the database

tasks.description:
Add the following to backend/loan-service/pom.xml under <dependencies>. No version attribute — managed by loanflow-parent:

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-redis</artifactId>
</dependency>

tasks.summary: Create CacheConfig bean with a dedicated TTL for application-details
Annotate getApplicationById with @Cacheable and status-update method with @CacheEvict

tasks.description:
Add the following to backend/loan-service/src/main/resources/application-dev.yaml:

spring:
  data:
    redis:
      host: localhost
      port: 6379
  cache:
    type: redis

For local runs without Redis, developers can override spring.cache.type=none to disable caching without changing application code.
